title: Consumer without idempotency — duplicate delivery causes double processing
expected_feedback: Critical: Consumer does not handle duplicate delivery. Kafka's at-least-once semantics guarantee that after a consumer rebalance, crash, or producer retry, the same message WILL be delivered again. This INSERT will either fail with a constraint violation (if order_id is unique) or create a duplicate record (if not unique). Fix: use idempotent database operations — INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING — or deduplicate by event_id before processing. Every Kafka consumer must be designed to handle duplicates.
